acceptance.rule: naive ≈ cmplog ≈ value_profile at GATE_BYTES=1; naive << {cmplog, value_profile} at GATE_BYTES=4; naive=0 while both cmplog>0 and value_profile>0 at GATE_BYTES=8

design_basis: A single contiguous-literal equality gate; the I2S/VP-vs-blind-search gap scales as 1/256^GATE_BYTES. cmplog substitutes the logged operand directly; value_profile's CMP_MAP buckets the wide compare's operand distance and climbs the Hamming gradient; naive must hit the full constant by chance. Both winners clear it independently — the 'independent' cluster identity.
notes: GATE_BYTES=1: naive expected to crash readily (~1/256 per exec); cmplog and value_profile similar — no axis advantage yet.
GATE_BYTES=2: naive ~1/65536 per exec, slow but possible; cmplog instant; value_profile fast via gradient.
GATE_BYTES=4: naive ~1/4G per exec, ~0-1 crash/600s; cmplog instant; value_profile clears via byte-by-byte CMP_MAP ascent.
GATE_BYTES=8: naive expected 0 crashes; cmplog instant; value_profile climbs the 8-byte Hamming gradient — both winners >0, naive=0.
